---
title: "Question 3"
---

We now turn to estimating the coverage of a percentile bootstrap confidence interval for a sample size of 50 sampled from an exponential distribution of mean 1.

```{r}
library(boot)
```

```{r}

set.seed(2025)  # For reproducibility

true_mean <- 1
n <- 50              # Sample size
B <- 1000            # Number of bootstrap resamples
n_sim <- 1000        # Number of simulations to check coverage

# Bootstrap function
boot_mean <- function(data, indices) {
  mean(data[indices])
}

# Track coverage
cover <- 0

for (i in 1:n_sim) {
  # Generate fresh sample from Exponential(1)
  sample_data <- rexp(n, rate = 1)
  
  # Perform bootstrap
  boot_result <- boot(sample_data, boot_mean, R = B)
  
  # Get percentile bootstrap CI
  ci <- boot.ci(boot_result, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  
  # Check if true mean is in CI
  if (!is.null(ci) && true_mean >= ci[1] && true_mean <= ci[2]) {
    cover <- cover + 1
  }
}

# Calculate coverage probability
coverage_prob <- cover / n_sim
cat("Coverage probability:", coverage_prob, "\n")
cat("Expected 95% CI coverage: 0.95\n")
cat("Difference:", abs(coverage_prob - 0.95))
```
